code:
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """Generate a natural language sentence from a list of triples."""
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Mapping predicates to phrase generators (object-focused, subject implied)
    def alt_name(s, o): return f"also known as {o}"
    def creator(s, o): return f"was created by {o}"
    def full_name(s, o): return f"whose full name is {o}"
    def founded_by(s, o): return f"was founded by {o}"
    def key_person(s, o): return f"with {o} as key person"
    def city(s, o): return f"located in {o}"
    def product(s, o): return f"produces {o}"
    def last_aired(s, o): return f"last aired on {o}"
    def starring(s, o): return f"starring {o}"
    def first_appearance(s, o): return f"first appeared in the film {o}"
    def voice(s, o): return f"voiced by {o}"
    def distributor(s, o): return f"distributed by {o}"
    def birth_place(s, o): return f"born in {o}"
    def nationality(s, o): return f"of {o} nationality"
    def award(s, o): return f"received the {o}"
    def broadcasted_by(s, o): return f"broadcasted by {o}"
    def first_aired(s, o): return f"first aired on {o}"
    def series(s, o): return f"part of the series {o}"
    def child(s, o): return f"has a child named {o}"

    phrase_funcs = {
        "alternativeName": alt_name,
        "creator": creator,
        "fullName": full_name,
        "foundedBy": founded_by,
        "keyPerson": key_person,
        "city": city,
        "product": product,
        "lastAired": last_aired,
        "starring": starring,
        "firstAppearanceInFilm": first_appearance,
        "voice": voice,
        "distributor": distributor,
        "birthPlace": birth_place,
        "nationality": nationality,
        "award": award,
        "broadcastedBy": broadcasted_by,
        "firstAired": first_aired,
        "series": series,
        "child": child,
    }

    # Group triples by subject
    subject_triples = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Track objects that are also subjects (for cross-references)
    all_subjects = set(subject_triples.keys())

    # Build complex sentence - focus on single subject case (most common for ComicsCharacter)
    if len(subject_triples) == 1:
        subject = list(subject_triples.keys())[0]
        triples_list = subject_triples[subject]

        # Group predicates for cleaner sentence construction
        creator_names = []
        other_phrases = []

        for t in triples_list:
            func = phrase_funcs.get(t.predicate)
            if t.predicate == "creator":
                creator_names.append(t.object)
            elif func:
                other_phrases.append((t.predicate, t.object, func(t.subject, t.object)))
            else:
                other_phrases.append((t.predicate, t.object, f"{t.predicate}: {t.object}"))

        # Build sentence with proper handling of multiple creators
        result_parts = []

        # Start with subject
        result_parts.append(subject)

        # Add creators if any
        if creator_names:
            if len(creator_names) == 1:
                result_parts.append(f" was created by {creator_names[0]}")
            else:
                creators_str = " and ".join(creator_names)
                result_parts.append(f" was created by {creators_str}")

        # Add other predicates as relative clauses
        for pred, obj, phrase in other_phrases:
            # Check if object is another subject (cross-reference)
            if obj in all_subjects:
                # This is a cross-reference - use relative clause
                result_parts.append(f", {phrase.lower()}")
            else:
                # Regular attribute - use relative clause
                result_parts.append(f", {phrase}")

        return " ".join(result_parts) + "."
    else:
        # Multiple subjects - handle with relative clauses to create one complex sentence
        subjects = list(subject_triples.keys())
        result_parts = []

        for i, subject in enumerate(subjects):
            triples_list = subject_triples[subject]

            # Group predicates
            creator_names = []
            other_phrases = []

            for t in triples_list:
                func = phrase_funcs.get(t.predicate)
                if t.predicate == "creator":
                    creator_names.append(t.object)
                elif func:
                    other_phrases.append((t.predicate, t.object, func(t.subject, t.object)))
                else:
                    other_phrases.append((t.predicate, t.object, f"{t.predicate}: {t.object}"))

            if i == 0:
                # First subject - start the sentence
                result_parts.append(subject)
                if creator_names:
                    if len(creator_names) == 1:
                        result_parts.append(f" was created by {creator_names[0]}")
                    else:
                        creators_str = " and ".join(creator_names)
                        result_parts.append(f" was created by {creators_str}")
                for pred, obj, phrase in other_phrases:
                    result_parts.append(f", {phrase}")
            else:
                # Subsequent subjects - use relative clause to connect
                if creator_names:
                    if len(creator_names) == 1:
                        result_parts.append(f", which was created by {creator_names[0]}")
                    else:
                        creators_str = " and ".join(creator_names)
                        result_parts.append(f", which was created by {creators_str}")
                for pred, obj, phrase in other_phrases:
                    result_parts.append(f", {phrase}")

        return " ".join(result_parts) + "."

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
